title: Alpha-numerical Codes and Error Detection
summary: Explains how computers encode characters (ASCII, EBCDIC, Unicode), introduces parity bits and Hamming code for error detection and correction.
content_points: Characters are assigned unique numbers; ASCII: 7 bits + 1 parity bit.
Unicode extends ASCII to cover non-Latin scripts.
Parity: 1 extra bit; detects a 1-bit error. Odd parity example: 110_2 (2 ones, even) → add 1 → send 1101.
Hamming code: 4 info bits + 3 parity bits; can detect/correct multiple errors simultaneously.
learning_objectives: Explain ASCII encoding and decode character values
Explain Hamming code structure and its advantages over parity
